title: cyanea lanceolata subsp. calycina
intro: Cyanea lanceolata subsp. calycina, also known as the Hawaiian lobelia, is a flowering plant species endemic to the Hawaiian Islands. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable resource in traditional Hawaiian medicine. Herbal preparations of Cyanea lanceolata subsp. calycina include teas, infusions, and topical applications, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
